expectations.evidence: Without-skill response correctly notes 'there is no .env file in the project yet' and blocks deployment

expectations.text: The agent copies .env.example to .env (or tells the user it will do this)
expectations.evidence: Response does not mention .env.example at all. It provides a generic template for the user to create manually — does not know about the existing .env.example in the skill directory

expectations.text: The agent does NOT run the deploy before the user confirms credentials are filled in
expectations.evidence: Response says 'Without authentication credentials, there is no way to connect' — correctly blocks deployment

expectations.text: The agent lists the four required variables: D365_URL, CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, TENANT_ID
expectations.evidence: Without-skill response lists D365_URL, CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, TENANT_ID (drawing on general D365 knowledge), plus optional SOLUTION_UNIQUE_NAME etc.

uncertainties: Does not know about the .env.example file in the project

eval_feedback.overall: The .env.example assertion is the most discriminating here — it catches the key gap where general knowledge doesn't surface project-specific shortcuts.
